notes: The doctrinally critical move in this verse is scope. Veda-vāda-rata is NOT 'devoted to the Vedas' (which would be a mistranslation pointing toward Vedic-rejection); it is 'engrossed in the (kāmya-karma) utterances of the Vedas'. The engineering layer preserves this scoping precisely: 'Krishna does not reject the kāmya-karma section of scripture; he rejects the reading that treats that section as the totality.' Avipaścit (undiscerning) is preserved as 'discrimination failure', not flattened to 'ignorant'. Puṣpitā vāc (flowery / blossomed-tree speech) is preserved with the bloomed-tree imagery intact. The 'na anyad asti' clause is correctly read as the diagnostic closure, not as a metaphysical claim about the Vedas. Both Shankara (engrossed in panegyric/fruits-of-action portions) and Ramanuja (rejoicing in the letter, attached to heaven) are preserved without conflation. PASS.
